title: The city bike scheme
explanation:
The city bike scheme

Three years ago, the city council introduced a bike-sharing scheme to reduce traffic and improve air quality. At first, residents were uncertain whether people would use it, but the number of journeys has increased every year. Users unlock bikes with a mobile app and can leave them at stations across the city. Although some people still prefer cars, the scheme has helped many commuters travel more cheaply and quickly, especially during rush hour.

questions.text: The bike scheme was introduced to reduce [*traffic|crime|noise from concerts].
Users unlock bikes with [*a mobile app|a paper ticket|cash].
The number of journeys has [*increased|fallen|stayed the same].
The scheme is especially useful during [*rush hour|holidays|the night].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The city council introduced the scheme ____ years ago. | *three | two | five
questions.feedback: The text says three years ago.

questions.text: People can leave bikes at stations across the ____.  | *city | country | road
questions.feedback: The stations are across the city.

questions.text: At first, some residents were ____.  | *uncertain | enthusiastic | angry
questions.feedback: They were uncertain whether it would work.

questions.text: The scheme helps people travel more cheaply and ____.  | *quickly | carefully | politely
The text is about [*public transport innovation|a sports competition|road safety laws only].
The writer presents the scheme as [*largely successful|a complete failure|too expensive to continue].
The scheme affects both [*transport and air quality|education and housing|law and crime].
Some people still [*prefer cars|do not own phones|avoid commuting].
questions.feedback: The passage notes that some still prefer cars.
